title: Spiral and Mixed Plaquette-Dimer Phases in the $S=1$ and $3/2$ Shastry-Sutherland Heisenberg Model
authors: Han-Qing Wu, Muwei Wu, Shou-Shu Gong
abstract: We investigate the ground-state phase diagram of the $S=1$ and $S=3/2$ Heisenberg model on the two-dimensional Shastry-Sutherland lattice (SSL) using density matrix renormalization group (DMRG) and cluster mean-field theory (CMFT). Between the dimer phase and Néel antiferromagnetic phases, we identify two intermediate phases: a mixed plaquette-dimer (MPD) phase and a spiral phase. These phases are characterized via bond energies and spin-spin correlation functions; phase boundaries are located from the ground-state energy derivative and entanglement entropy. The MPD phase exhibits strong intradimer correlations and weak tetramerization on the empty plaquettes, and its transitions to the dimer and spiral phases are first order. Combining our results with the known boundaries for $S=1/2$ and the classical limit $S\to\infty$, we construct a global $S$-$g$ phase diagram. This diagram reveals the progressive suppression of quantum effects with increasing $S$ and offers a theoretical framework for larger-$S$ SSL materials.
summary: This paper explores how increasing the spin magnitude in the Shastry-Sutherland model affects its complex phase diagram. It discovers that higher spin values stabilize a spiral phase while suppressing the mixed plaquette-dimer phase, providing a bridge between quantum and classical magnetism.
main_problem: The study investigates the evolution of the ground-state phase diagram of the Shastry-Sutherland Heisenberg model from the extreme quantum limit (S=1/2) toward the classical limit (S to infinity), specifically focusing on intermediate phases for S=1 and S=3/2.
main_result: The authors identify two intermediate phases, a mixed plaquette-dimer (MPD) phase and a spiral phase, between the dimer and Neel antiferromagnetic phases, and show that the spiral phase expands while the MPD phase shrinks as spin magnitude increases.
method: The study utilizes Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) on cylinders and Cluster Mean-Field Theory (CMFT) with DMRG as an impurity solver.
model_or_system: The system is the two-dimensional Shastry-Sutherland lattice (SSL) described by an isotropic Heisenberg antiferromagnet with nearest-neighbor (inter-dimer) coupling J and diagonal (intra-dimer) coupling J'.
key_observables: Bond energies, spin-spin correlation functions, bipartite entanglement entropy, second derivative of ground-state energy, and the static spin structure factor S(q).
important_parameters: Spin magnitudes S=1 and S=3/2; coupling ratio g = J/J'.
main_assumptions: The study assumes an isotropic Heisenberg model and relies on the accuracy of DMRG and CMFT approximations on finite-width cylinders.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the cylinder geometry and a global S-g phase diagram; Figure 2 illustrates spin textures for S=1; Figure 3 displays energy second derivatives, entanglement entropy, and real-space spin correlations for S=3/2.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Shastry-Sutherland model, details the DMRG and CMFT methodologies, presents the phase identification for S=1 and S=3/2, discusses the evolution of phases toward the classical limit, and concludes with a global phase diagram.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Fig. 1(b). For S = 1/2, we adopt the transition points gc1 = 0.676 and gc2 = 0.8 from Ref. [20]. The proposed quantum spin liquid (QSL) regime is indicated in purple (see, e.g., Refs. [10–12, 18, 19] and references therein). For S ≥1, a spiral phase emerges between the MPD and NAF phases. As S increases, the spiral region expands continuously, while the MPD phase shrinks and shifts to- ward smaller g until it disappears; the dimer phase like- wise diminishes and vanishes in the classical limit. In the S →∞limit, the phase diagram reduces to the classi- cal case, with only the spiral and NAF phases.
